sanskrit: स घोषो धार्तराष्ट्राणां हृदयानि व्यदारयत्। नभश्च पृथिवीं चैव तुमुलो व्यनुनादयन्॥
transliteration: sa ghoṣo dhārtarāṣṭrāṇāṃ hṛdayāni vyadarayat nabhaś ca pṛthivīṃ caiva tumulo 'bhyanunādayan
literal_meaning: That uproarious sound shattered the hearts of the sons of Dhṛtarāṣṭra, resounding through sky and earth.
contextual_meaning: The Pāṇḍavas’ conch-blast is not mere war-cry; it is an existential mirror that makes the Kauravas confront the hollowness of their own cause and the inevitability of their downfall. Their hearts break not from physical fear but from the sudden, unwanted clarity that their identity as righteous rulers has been a self-deception.
psychological_state: The moment when a person’s defensive narrative cracks and the ego hears the reverberation of its own falsity.
trigger_scenario: A corporate whistle-blower stands outside the boardroom, hears the confident voices of ethical colleagues, and suddenly feels the ground of self-justification give way.
anchor_text: Hollow causes echo loudest when truth sounds.
